description_long: This is a compact, dome-shaped tent with a muted olive green exterior, designed for outdoor use in urban parks or temporary street setups. The tent features a single, triangular front entrance outlined with yellow piping, which likely serves as a zippered door for easy access. The structure is supported by flexible poles that create a rounded, aerodynamic profile, ideal for stability and wind resistance. The fabric appears to be a durable, weather-resistant synthetic material, possibly polyester or nylon, with reinforced seams along the edges. There are no visible windows, mesh panels, or additional ventilation openings, suggesting a simple, utilitarian design. The tent sits low to the ground and is anchored at the corners, with no visible branding or decorative elements.
description: A small, olive green dome tent with a triangular front entrance outlined in yellow, made from weather-resistant fabric and supported by flexible poles.
description_view_0: Side view showing the smooth, uninterrupted olive green fabric with no visible entrance or windows.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the triangular entrance outlined in yellow piping, indicating the main access point.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with no visible openings or features.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the rounded back of the tent, with no visible doors or windows.
attribute_entrance_type: zippered door at the front, triangular shape outlined with yellow piping, single main entrance
attribute_guy_line_configuration: No visible guy lines; tent appears to be anchored at the four corners only.
